originalTitle: Deadly Sudan drone strike targets funeral procession
title: Deadly Sudan drone strike targets funeral procession
content: Rights groups accuse the RSF paramilitary group of killing civilians in el-Obeid, a city on the civil war's front line.

originalTitle: Nigeria evacuates citizens from South Africa as anti-migrant sentiment rises
title: Nigeria evacuates citizens from South Africa as anti-migrant sentiment rises
content: Nigeria is the latest African state to repatriate citizens following reports of xenophobic attacks.

originalTitle: Artan to referee Uefa Super Cup after losing World Cup role
title: Artan to referee Uefa Super Cup after losing World Cup role
content: Somali referee Omar Artan is appointed to take charge of the Uefa Super Cup match between Paris St-Germain and Aston Villa later this summer after being denied a spot at the World Cup.

originalTitle: Bosnia’s Esmir Bajraktarevic: Child of Srebrenica
title: Esmir's Amazing Football Journey!
content:
Hello, Little Sports Fans!

Hello, super sports fans! Welcome to your special news from Africa! Today, we have a very exciting story just for you. It’s all about a young man who loves football so much. His name is Esmir Bajraktarevic (say that with me: Es-meer Bye-rak-ta-re-vich!). Esmir is getting ready for a very, very big game. This game is part of a huge football party called the World Cup!

Meet Esmir! Our Football Hero

Esmir is a talented football player. He is quite young, but he plays like a superstar! He started kicking a ball when he was just a little boy. He always dreamed of playing football for his country. Esmir comes from a country far away called Bosnia and Herzegovina. It is a beautiful place in Europe. He has worked very, very hard to be where he is today. Practicing every day helped him become a great player.

Football is Fun for Everyone!

Do you love to play with a ball? Football is a super fun game! Two teams try to kick the ball into each other's net. It makes us run, jump, and laugh a lot. Esmir loves everything about football. He loves scoring goals and helping his friends on the team. Playing football helps him stay strong and happy. It's also a great way to make new friends!

Bosnia and Herzegovina: Esmir's Home Country

Esmir is very proud to play for Bosnia and Herzegovina. It's a special country with lovely mountains and rivers. The people there love football very much! His country's football team has a cool nickname. They are called the “Dragons”! Imagine dragons playing football! Esmir wears a special jersey with his country’s flag on it when he plays. *Did you know?* The flag of Bosnia and Herzegovina is blue with a big yellow triangle and white stars! It looks very bright!

The Amazing World Cup!

The World Cup is the biggest football party in the whole wide world! All the best football teams from different countries come together. They play exciting games to see who is the champion. It happens only every four years. So, it's a very special time for football fans everywhere! Everyone cheers loudly for their favourite teams. It's full of fun and excitement for children and grown-ups. *Did you know?* The first World Cup was played over 90 years ago, in 1930! That’s even before your grandparents were born!

Bosnia's Dragons are Ready!

Esmir and his team, the Bosnia and Herzegovina Dragons, are getting ready for their first big game. They will play against another team from a country called Canada. This game is very important! All eyes will be on Esmir during the match. He is a key player for his team. He hopes to play his very best and help his team win. Making his country proud is his biggest wish.

Esmir's Special Story: From Srebrenica

Esmir has a very special story. His family comes from a town called Srebrenica (say that: Sreb-re-nee-tsa). Srebrenica is a town in Bosnia and Herzegovina. It is a very important place for his family. Esmir’s family made a brave decision to move to a new country for a fresh start. They wanted to find new opportunities. But Esmir always remembers where his family came from. He carries the spirit of Srebrenica and his family's history with him. When he plays football, he plays for his family, for his country, and for his special hometown. It gives him extra strength and courage. *Simple Explanation:* Your “hometown” is the town where you or your family lived when they were growing up. It’s a place full of special memories and history!

Why Does This Matter to Esmir?

Playing for his country means everything to Esmir. He wants to show everyone what is possible with hard work and determination. He plays with all his heart in every game. Esmir wants to bring joy to the people of Bosnia and Herzegovina. He wants to show that no matter what, you can always hope and dream. His football journey is a message of hope for everyone.

Dreaming Big Like Esmir

Esmir is a wonderful role model for boys and girls all over Africa and the world. He shows us that if you have a dream and work hard, you can make it come true. Never give up on your dreams! He inspires many young footballers to keep practicing. He teaches us to be proud of where we come from. Esmir reminds us to always give our best, on and off the field. *Simple Explanation:* A “role model” is someone you look up to and want to be like! They show us how to be good and do great things!

The Big Game Day is Coming!

The day of the big game against Canada is getting closer! Esmir and his teammates are training very hard every day. They are ready to show their amazing football skills to the world. Everyone in Bosnia and Herzegovina will be watching and cheering loudly. We will all be wishing Esmir and the Dragons good luck! We hope they play their best game ever.

Football: A Game for Everyone!

Esmir's story teaches us that football is more than just a game. It brings people together from all different places. It's about teamwork, friendship, and celebrating who we are. We are so excited to see Esmir play! Keep dreaming big, little sports fans. Maybe one day, you too will play in a big game like Esmir! Hooray for football and hooray for Esmir!
summary: Esmir Bajraktarevic, a young football star from Bosnia and Herzegovina, is ready to play in the big World Cup game against Canada, bringing hope and pride to his country and his special hometown of Srebrenica.
whyMatters: This story matters because Esmir is a young boy who worked hard to follow his dream to play football for his country, showing all children that with practice and pride in your family's history, you can achieve amazing things and inspire many people.

title: World Cup kicks off in Mexico with Shakira, dancing, and protests
content: There was a star-studded opening ceremony, and some sporadic clashes between police and protesters around the Azteca stadium.

originalTitle: From near death to World Cup hero - Jimenez's tears for Mexico
title: From near death to World Cup hero - Jimenez's tears for Mexico
content: From nearly dying on the pitch to scoring for Mexico at the World Cup - Raul Jimenez has completed a remarkable comeback.

originalTitle: Three red cards - are referees getting tough at this World Cup?
title: Three red cards - are referees getting tough at this World Cup?
content: Three players were sent off in the World Cup opener as Mexico beat South Africa 2-0. Are we about to see a World Cup littered with red cards?

originalTitle: Three red cards shown as Mexico beat South Africa in World Cup opener
title: Three red cards shown as Mexico beat South Africa in World Cup opener
content: Three players are sent off as World Cup co-hosts Mexico defeat South Africa in a dramatic opening match at a packed Azteca Stadium.
